issues.claim: engineering layer's treatment of metaphysical layering; engineering analog stays at four-layer attention model
issues.objection: The engineering body preserves Sanskrit terminology (kāma, kṛtsna-vit, ātmānam ātmanā, etc.) and does not misattribute traditions. The STRETCHED tag is appropriate for the metaphysical content. Lexically clean: tradition-specific terms (saṅkara, paripanthinau, sarva-jñāna-vimūḍha) carry their technical force in context.

notes: Verse BG 3.42: source pack triangulates 5 sources (vedabase.io, holy-bhagavad-gita.org, gitasupersite.iitk.ac.in, bhagavad-gita.us, GRETIL). Sanskrit Devanagari + IAST agree with vedabase canonical text. Anvaya complete with proper compound splitting. Translation by Prabhupada captured verbatim; Mukundananda cross-referenced. Shankara/Ramanuja excerpts captured fair-use with verbatim_excerpt_length recorded. Disagreements section names a specific lexical disagreement and explains tradition divergence honestly. Engineering layer preserves Sanskrit anchors (metaphysical layering; engineering analog stays at four-layer attention model) and does not flatten to generic prose. The engineering layer is correctly tagged STRETCHED with a documented gap between the doctrinal claim and the operational analog; this is honest scope-management. Pass.
